name: Avoid using jQuery 3.x deprecated methods
description:
Be careful when using deprecated methods. The deprecation period should be used to smoothly make the transition. The rule raises an issue when any of the following methods is used:
- $("#foo").bind()
- $("#foo").unbind()
- $("#foo").delegate()
- $("#foo").undelegate()
- jQuery.parseJSON()
- jQuery.unique()
- jQuery.fx.interval
- jQuery.isFunction()
- jQuery.isNumeric()
- jQuery.now()
- jQuery.trim()
output:
Associated to each violation, the following information is provided:
- The number of violation occurrences
- Bookmarks for violation occurrences found in the source code
rationale:
Using deprecated jQuery 3.x API could lead to security compliancy issues.
Deprecated jQuery API may be removed in a future major-version update, or it is already removed.
reference:
jQuery API Documentation :
https://api.jquery.com/

jQuery Core 3.0 Upgrade Guide :
https://jquery.com/upgrade-guide/3.0/
https://api.jquery.com/category/deprecated/deprecated-3.0/
remediation:
Replace the deprecated API function call by the new one, when applicable.  the JQuery documentation provides the remediations. For instance:
.bind(), .unbind(), .delegate(), .undelegate() (deprecated 3.0) → use .on() / .off().
jQuery.parseJSON() (deprecated 3.0) → use JSON.parse.
jQuery.unique() (deprecated 3.0) → use jQuery.uniqueSort().
jQuery.fx.interval (deprecated 3.0).
jQuery.isFunction(), jQuery.isNumeric(), jQuery.now() (deprecated 3.3) → use typeof x === "function", Number checks/Number.isFinite, and Date.now().
jQuery.trim() (deprecated 3.5) → use String.prototype.trim().
total: Number of JavaScript source codes using jQuery
alternativeName: Ensure you don't use officially jQuery deprecated methods (before 3.x)

qualityStandards.name: Ensure all Systems and Software are Protected from Known Vulnerabilities
Ensure all Systems and Software are Protected from Known Vulnerabilities
An inventory of bespoke and custom software, and third-party software components incorporated into bespoke and custom software is maintained to facilitate vulnerability and patch management.
